backtest_summary.dedupe_explanation: Repeated 15-minute AWS scans are counted once per target, market, and side by default, using the latest pre-resolution row.

challenger.reason: clean-blend/combined has 8 outcome rows; needs more than 180 rows for this walk-forward comparison.

comparison.label: Not enough clean challenger data yet
comparison.recommendation: Keep AWS execution pinned to lstm.

learnings: NO side: 0/2 won, net $-0.95 this window.
YES side: 0/1 won, net $-0.05 this window.
Most frequent gate rejections: spread (2674x); edge (410x).
Clean next-day forecast error averaged 1.23F across 6 settled day(s) in this window.
recommended_changes: Sample is small (3 resolved trades); treat window ROI as noise and wait for at least 15 resolved trades before changing gates.

action_items: Keep nightly dataset backfill running until each candidate has 30 matched settled highs.
Backfill or enable Kalshi trade history before using datasets for trading weight.
Keep live AWS execution pinned to LSTM until both accuracy and market gates pass.

dataset_research.headline: Dataset collection is live, but forecast features are not yet matched to settled SFO highs.

profitability_gate.reason: No dataset source gets live trading weight until it has an after-cost market backtest with at least 30 matched trades. Current collected trade rows: 0.

dataset_research.promotion_rule: Collect broadly, but do not give a new source live model weight or loosen paper-trading gates until it improves held-out forecast error and then survives an after-cost market backtest with enough trades.

disclaimer: Paper-trading research only. The active AWS execution calibration remains pinned to lstm; this artifact does not place live orders.

Using information that was not available at trade time; Strategy Lab separates pre-resolution rows.
A signal recorded before market close or an observed-high lock.
Repeated 15-minute scans are reduced to one sampled row per target, market, and side.
How closely stated probabilities match observed win frequencies.
Squared probability error; lower is better.
Penalty for assigning low probability to what happened; lower is better.
Simulated positions recorded for research. No live money is placed.
A research calibration compared against the active execution calibration.
